key_insight: Generative models have a fixed "steering budget": knobs can only move part of a property range, while concrete examples can reach the rest, often larger part.
engineering_relevance: For materials generation, example-based steering can achieve property targets and structural patterns that prompts or tags cannot, improving controllability in crystal design.
method: Audit the training data to estimate each property's steerable budget before training.
Split steering into knob-reachable and example-only regions.
Build an example set from learned model behavior to cover the full budget, including targets that are hard to express in words.
evidence_signal: The paper validates the budget idea with falsifiable tests in two domains—image generation and crystal-structure generation—showing when knobs suffice and when examples are needed.
watch_item: The example-based approach depends on the quality and representativeness of the training data budget, so limited or biased data may reduce reachable control.
paper_title: The Steering Budget: Examples beat Knobs
